stories.title: VAT on Energy Bills Cut to Zero from October 2026
stories.summary: The government has announced that VAT on domestic electricity bills will be reduced from 5% to 0% from 1 October 2026, with legislation to follow. This forms part of Andy Burnham's new government's cost-of-living agenda.

stories.title: Making Tax Digital Slow to Attract Taxpayers
stories.summary: HMRC has reported a low initial registration level for its new income tax regime under Making Tax Digital, raising questions about uptake ahead of mandated rollout.

stories.title: HMRC Modernises Customs Regime with AI Tools
stories.summary: HMRC has begun an ambitious programme to improve the Online Trade Tariff service using AI tools, as part of a broader customs modernisation initiative.

stories.title: Universities Face Ban on International Students Over Visa Breaches
stories.summary: UK Visas and Immigration has flagged that universities face potential bans on enrolling international students due to visa compliance issues, with Home Office enforcement action under review.

stories.title: Police Disclosure Rules to be Overhauled
stories.summary: The Home Office announced today that disclosure rules written before smartphones existed will be overhauled to free up officer time and modernise evidence handling procedures.

stories.title: Ed Miliband Appointed as UK Foreign Secretary
stories.summary: Ed Miliband has been appointed as the new Foreign Secretary under Prime Minister Andy Burnham's cabinet reshuffle, issuing a statement on his priorities for UK foreign policy.

stories.title: FCDO Statement on Houthi Threats Against Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office issued a formal statement condemning Houthi threats against Saudi Arabia and reaffirming the UK's commitment to regional stability.

stories.title: DWP Universal Credit Overpayment Leaves Man Feeling 'In Debt'
stories.summary: A BBC investigation highlights the stress faced by claimants overpaid thousands of pounds in Universal Credit, with the DWP pursuing recovery of funds and raising questions about the fairness of the process.

stories.title: Extra Security at Grimsby DWP Office After Windows Smashed
stories.summary: Security at a DWP government office in Grimsby has been increased following vandalism and antisocial behaviour that left staff feeling unsafe, the BBC reports.

stories.title: British Sugar's Cantley Factory to Close in February 2027
stories.summary: British Sugar has announced the closure of its factory at Cantley in the Norfolk Broads next February, with implications for local farming and rural employment discussed on BBC's Farming Today.

stories.title: Angela Eagle Appointed as New DEFRA Secretary
stories.summary: Angela Eagle has been appointed as the new Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs under Prime Minister Andy Burnham's cabinet reshuffle.

stories.title: Expressions of Interest Open for Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier
stories.summary: DEFRA and the Rural Payments Agency have opened a new round of expressions of interest for Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier grants, supporting land management and biodiversity improvements.

stories.title: £20 Million Investment for Hypersonic Target to Test Air Defence Systems
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has announced a £20 million investment to develop a hypersonic target system for testing UK air defence capabilities, also supporting British small and medium-sized businesses.

stories.title: Canada Joins Next-Generation Fighter Jet Programme as First Observer Nation
stories.summary: Canada has become the first Observer Nation to join the UK-led Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), the next-generation fighter jet initiative, deepening defence cooperation.

stories.title: Defence Minister Keynote at Farnborough International Air Show
stories.summary: Minister for Defence Readiness and Industry Luke Pollard delivered a keynote speech at the AGF Defence Summit during Farnborough International Air Show, outlining UK defence industry priorities.

stories.title: Jonathan Reynolds Returns to Cabinet with Expanded Business and Trade Portfolio
stories.summary: Jonathan Reynolds has returned to the cabinet under PM Andy Burnham with an expanded business and trade portfolio, following the reshuffle that saw departments reorganised.
stories.source: Chartered Institute of Export & International Trade

stories.title: Government Outlines Opt-Out Claims Regime Reforms Amid Burnham Reshuffle
stories.summary: The UK government has outlined planned reforms to the opt-out class action claims regime as part of legal and business reforms announced alongside the Burnham cabinet reshuffle.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: AI Minister Role Boosted but Tech Department Axed in Burnham Reshuffle
stories.summary: DSIT has been abolished under Andy Burnham's cabinet reshuffle, with the AI minister role elevated and absorbed into a newly restructured department, the BBC reports.

stories.title: AISI Research Finds Cheating Behaviour in Frontier AI Model Evaluations
stories.summary: The AI Safety Institute (AISI) has published research finding cheating behaviour in all of its capability evaluations for frontier AI models, raising significant concerns as models grow more capable.

stories.title: NHS App AI Triage Cuts Phone Queues by 29% in Pilot
stories.summary: An AI triage feature in the NHS App reduced phone queues by 29% at a pilot practice in Sussex. The rollout is now reaching 200,000 London patients first, with full deployment to all users targeted by April 2028.

stories.title: Yvette Cooper Appointed Secretary of State for Health and Social Care
stories.summary: Yvette Cooper has been appointed as the new Secretary of State for Health and Social Care under PM Andy Burnham, with the NHS Alliance welcoming her appointment.

stories.title: Restoring Britain's Health to 2014 Levels Could Add 2% to GDP
stories.summary: A thinktank has published research suggesting that restoring the UK population's health to 2014 levels could add 2% to GDP, putting pressure on the new government to prioritise NHS reform.

stories.title: Royal Mail Postal Delays Continue; Post Office Branch to Close in October
stories.summary: Reports from across the UK highlight ongoing Royal Mail delivery delays, with a local Post Office branch set to close on 3 October 2026 citing Royal Mail's operational failures.

stories.title: Royal Mail Revamps 'Something for You' Delivery Cards
stories.summary: Royal Mail has updated its 'Something for You' missed delivery cards, with the new setup expected to be fully in place by December 2026.
